source.name: Canada.ca — Study permit: Proof of financial support

extraSources.name: Canada.ca — Study permit: How to apply

requirements.label: Letter of acceptance (LOA)
requirements.detail: An acceptance letter from a Designated Learning Institution (DLI).

requirements.label: Provincial/Territorial Attestation Letter (PAL/TAL)
requirements.detail: Most study permit applicants now need a PAL/TAL from the province or territory.

requirements.label: Proof of financial support
requirements.detail: Show at least CAD 22,895 for living expenses (outside Quebec), separate from first-year tuition and travel. A GIC is one accepted way to prove this.

requirements.label: Health insurance
requirements.detail: Provincial coverage varies; arrange private/student health insurance where you aren't covered.

requirements.label: English/French language proof
requirements.detail: IELTS/TOEFL/PTE (or French tests) as your institution requires.

requirements.label: Biometrics
requirements.detail: Most applicants must give fingerprints and a photo at a Visa Application Centre.

requirements.label: Valid passport
requirements.detail: A passport valid for your intended period of study.

requirements.label: Medical exam
requirements.detail: Required depending on your country of residence and length of stay.

requirements.label: GIC / tuition transfer
requirements.detail: Fund a GIC or transfer tuition with a low-FX provider.

requirements.label: Connectivity on arrival
requirements.detail: An eSIM gives you a Canadian number and data on landing.

requirements.label: Accommodation
requirements.detail: Arrange housing early, especially in major cities.
